description.task: Implement Merge Sort from scratch using the divide-and-conquer strategy. Split the array in half, recursively sort each half, then merge them. Use an auxiliary array for the merge step.
instructions: The copy-back loop is already provided
Merge sort is O(n log n) in all cases and is stable

description.task: Implement Quick Sort using the Lomuto partition scheme. Choose the last element as the pivot, partition the array, and recursively sort the two partitions. Print the pivot and partition result at each step.
instructions: Pivot is arr[high]=70 for arr1; after partition, 70 ends up at its correct sorted position
Quick sort is O(n log n) on average but O(n²) worst case (sorted input with last-element pivot)
Remove the return -1 line and replace with the correct return statement
description.hint: if(arr[j]<=pivot){i++;int t=arr[i];arr[i]=arr[j];arr[j]=t;} Then: int t=arr[i+1];arr[i+1]=arr[high];arr[high]=t; return i+1;

description.task: Implement Heap Sort. First build a max-heap from the array, then repeatedly extract the maximum (swap root with last unsorted element) and heapify down. Implement the heapify helper method.
instructions: Build-heap pass converts: {12,11,13,5,6,7} into a max-heap
Heap sort is O(n log n) in all cases and sorts in-place but is NOT stable
